phase_learnings.phase_name: Fix failing test 42: eternal promotion strength check

learnings.claim: Test fixtures with hardcoded dates will break as calendar time advances — use dynamic date computation with cross-platform fallbacks instead
learnings.evidence: Test 42 failed because 2024-01-01 fixture was 817 days old, decaying FOCUS effective_strength to 0. Dynamic dates (1 day ago) give eff=0.87, well above 0.80 threshold.

phase_learnings.phase_name: Verify today new test files and implementations

learnings.claim: Shell functions that set traps must compose with _aether_exit_cleanup to avoid orphaning file locks and temp files when the function exits abnormally
learnings.evidence: Auditor found queen.sh:406 and midden.sh:280 traps overwrote _aether_exit_cleanup — fixed by composing cleanup in trap handler

learnings.claim: Shell functions that embed user-derived values in JSON output strings must use jq for safe construction to prevent JSON injection from special characters
learnings.evidence: Auditor found queen.sh:1291 colony_name and queen.sh:642 promote output interpolated user input without escaping — fixed with jq -n --arg

phase_learnings.phase_name: Audit QUEEN.md and wisdom pipeline integrity

learnings.claim: Shell functions using sed c-command for line replacement must use head/tail instead to handle multi-line content safely on macOS BSD sed
learnings.evidence: sed c-command produced empty output on macOS when entry variable contained newlines, silently overwriting QUEEN.md with 0 bytes. head/tail pattern from lines 917-922 handles multi-line content correctly.

learnings.claim: Every atomic mv that overwrites a critical file must have a non-empty size guard to prevent data destruction from upstream pipeline failures
learnings.evidence: 5 mv operations in queen.sh could overwrite QUEEN.md with empty content if any preceding awk/sed/head-tail pipeline produced empty output. Adding if [[ ! -s $tmp_file ]] guard prevents data loss.

learnings.claim: Lifecycle commands (init, seal, entomb) properly handle colony_version through template system — init uses colony-state.template.json, seal has 12 references, entomb reads and displays it
learnings.evidence: Grep confirmed init.md references colony-state.template.json which contains colony_version:1, seal.md has 12 colony_version references, entomb.md has 5 references including display formatting

learnings.claim: JSON injection in spawn.sh persists despite being identified as an instinct — pattern recurs because the fix was applied only to queen.sh, not spawn.sh
learnings.evidence: Auditor Inspect-60 found spawn.sh:34,64,134,140,167,191 embed variables directly into JSON strings. Colony instinct at confidence 0.8 already identifies this pattern but fix was not applied to spawn.sh

phase_learnings.phase_name: Stabilize spawn-tree parsing and JSON output

learnings.claim: Replacing bash while-read+sed loops with single-pass awk eliminates O(n^2) subprocess forking — 4000+ forks reduced to 1 awk process, runtime from 23s to 1.7s
learnings.evidence: Watcher confirmed 9/9 spawn-tree tests pass, 616/616 full suite pass, spawn-tree-load returns valid JSON with 45 spawns

learnings.claim: awk NF==7 for spawn detection and $3 match for completion detection handles pipes-in-summary edge cases that pipe-counting cannot
learnings.evidence: Chaos ant confirmed completion lines with || in summary are correctly parsed via $3 content check rather than field count

learnings.claim: Audit reports must use actual test output counts, not builder-claimed counts — always cross-verify bash test pass rates against live test-aether-utils.sh output
learnings.evidence: Builder reported 42/43 bash tests passing; actual run showed 37/43 (12 failures in dual-mode). Chaos ant caught the discrepancy.

instincts.trigger: shell scripts use sed c-command for line replacement
instincts.action: replace with head/tail pattern for cross-platform newline safety — sed c breaks on macOS BSD with multi-line content
evidence: queen.sh sed c-command produced empty output on macOS when entry contained newlines, overwriting QUEEN.md with 0 bytes

instincts.trigger: atomic mv overwrites a critical data file
instincts.action: add non-empty size guard (if [[ ! -s file ]]) before mv to prevent data destruction from upstream pipeline failures
evidence: 5 mv operations in queen.sh could silently destroy QUEEN.md content if preceding awk/sed pipeline produced empty output

instincts.trigger: bash scripts use while-read+sed/cut loops for file parsing
instincts.action: replace with single-pass awk to eliminate O(n^2) subprocess forking — awk has associative arrays even when bash 3.2 does not
evidence: spawn-tree.sh: 4000+ subprocess forks reduced to 1 awk process, runtime 23s to 1.7s

instincts.trigger: builder produces audit or summary report with test metrics
instincts.action: cross-verify all pass/fail counts against live test output before accepting report as complete — builder claims are not authoritative
evidence: Builder reported 42/43 bash tests passing; live run showed 37/43. Chaos ant caught the 5-test discrepancy.

instincts.action: replace with dynamic cross-platform date computation to prevent time-based test degradation
evidence: Test 42 broke because hardcoded 2024 dates decayed effective_strength to 0 over 817 days

instincts.action: compose trap with _aether_exit_cleanup to preserve lock and temp file cleanup on abnormal exit
evidence: Auditor caught queen.sh and midden.sh trap overrides orphaning cleanup — fixed by composing

instincts.trigger: resilience testing (chaos ant) finds data accuracy issues in builder output
instincts.action: always cross-verify builder claims against live test/command output before accepting — chaos findings on data accuracy should be treated as blockers
evidence: 4 resilience failures recorded in midden; chaos ant found 3 data accuracy issues including misstated bash test pass rate

instincts.action: Chaos resilience moderate: 5 scenarios tested on pheromone-expire date fix, 3 resilient, 2 findings (static fallback staleness, double date failure)
Test fixtures with hardcoded dates will break as calendar time advances — use dynamic date computation with cross-platform fallbacks instead
Shell functions that set traps must compose with _aether_exit_cleanup to avoid orphaning file locks and temp files when the function exits abnormally
Shell functions that embed user-derived values in JSON output strings must use jq for safe construction to prevent JSON injection from special characters
Shell functions using sed c-command for line replacement must use head/tail instead to handle multi-line content safely on macOS BSD sed
Every atomic mv that overwrites a critical file must have a non-empty size guard to prevent data destruction from upstream pipeline failures
Lifecycle commands (init, seal, entomb) properly handle colony_version through template system — init uses colony-state.template.json, seal has 12 references, entomb reads and displays it
JSON injection in spawn.sh persists despite being identified as an instinct — pattern recurs because the fix was applied only to queen.sh, not spawn.sh

Inserted phase 6 (Stabilize spawn-tree parsing and JSON output): Fix all 3 spawn-tree blockers: (1) Fix O(n^2) parse_spawn_tree scaling that causes test timeouts, (2) JSON-escape ant names before interpolation into JSON output strings, (3) Add structural JSON validation in spawn-tree-load before passing through json_ok
Chaos resilience strong: 4/5 scenarios resilient for spawn-tree awk rewrite, 1 medium finding on newline edge case
Replacing bash while-read+sed loops with single-pass awk eliminates O(n^2) subprocess forking
awk NF==7 for spawn detection and $3 match for completion detection handles pipes-in-summary edge cases
Audit reports must use actual test output counts, not builder-claimed counts — always cross-verify bash test pass rates against live test-aether-utils.sh output
Prompt injection regex needs multi-word support with optional groups
Colony-prime token budget 8000 chars matches measured output of 2000-5000 chars
Two wisdom extraction functions must be updated in lockstep
Hub-level shared files need hub-level locks — per-repo LOCK_DIR cannot protect cross-repo resources
Content sanitization pattern from pheromone-write is reusable for any user-supplied text input
Orchestrator commands should delegate to sibling subcommands via bash $0 rather than duplicating logic
Text transformation placeholders should avoid angle brackets to prevent double-escaping
Domain filtering in colony-prime uses hive-read with domain_tags from registry
Chaos resilience strong: 5 scenarios tested, hive promotion handles empty instincts, special chars in paths, boundary confidence values correctly
Archaeology pre-build scans catch API contract mismatches (--instinct vs --text) that would silently fail at runtime
